violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Handsink observed to be leaking, and the cold water was turned off. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets have been ordered and are expected today. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

The floor in a couple of areas of the kitchen is in poor repair. There is exposed wood. Wood is not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Materials to repair the flooring have been obtained. Flooring is to be repaired using proper materials. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Chicken, which was prepared yesterday, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Temperature observed at 43 - 48 degrees F. Voluntary disposal during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
A couple of spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed in the 1-door reach-in refrigerator in hotel pans that were tightly packed and stacked on top on each other. Proper cooling reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2024
One of the refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gasket must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024
Faucet of the handsink is in poor repair. One of the faucet handles is not able to be turned. Plumbing must remain in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

The floor in a couple of areas in the kitchen and the wall to the side of the 3-bay sink are in poor repair. There is exposed wood. Wood is not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in observed in poor repair. New gaskets have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Employees were observed performing various activities and changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Food debris observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Chicken, which was prepared yesterday, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Chicken observed with a temperature of 46 degrees F. Voluntary disposal during inspection. Proper cooling methods discussed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Raw meat observed with a temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit in the 3-door lowboy unit. Ambient temperature of this unit observed at 35 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Meat was divided into smaller pans and returned to unit. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed warm in the refrigerator in a container greater than four inches deep. Proper cooling methods discussed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Splashguard is needed at the bar between the handsink and the dump sink to prevent contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Large amount of ice observed in the small freezer unit in contact with various food products. Freezer must be defrosted to eliminate the ice. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 3-door reach-in were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the door. Gaskets must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2023

The floor in a couple of areas is in poor repair (exposed wood). Wood is not smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
